game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Plague Inc. Plague Inc. is a strategy simulation where players evolve a pathogen to infect and eradicate humanity before a cure is developed, spending "DNA points" to upgrade transmission methods, symptoms, and resistances. The game simulates global infection spread on a world map, factoring in country responses, research efforts, and public awareness, with difficulty levels impacting cure speed and starting conditions.
game_info.description: Pathogen Pandemic is a strategy game where you control a deadly pathogen trying to infect the entire world. Evolve your pathogen by spending DNA points to improve transmission, symptoms, and resistances while racing against scientists developing a cure. Infect all countries before the cure is completed to win, but be careful - if the cure finishes first or all infected countries are completely isolated, you lose!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between upgrades in the menu
SPACE: Select/purchase an upgrade when highlighted
Z: Toggle between world map and upgrade menu views
SHIFT: Accelerate game time (2x speed)
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart game when on game over screen
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic game functionality by ensuring the game loads without errors and responds properly to random control inputs.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Periodically switches between random actions including toggling views, selecting upgrades, and accelerating time.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should run continuously without errors, allowing interaction with all basic game mechanics.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests the win condition by implementing an optimal strategy to infect the entire world before the cure completes.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Strategically purchases transmission upgrades first, followed by resistance upgrades, while managing DNA points efficiently.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The pathogen should successfully infect all countries before the cure reaches 100%, triggering the win condition.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the loss condition by deliberately avoiding transmission upgrades to allow the cure to complete.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Focuses exclusively on symptom upgrades while ignoring transmission, allowing the cure to progress faster than global infection.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The cure should reach 100% completion before all countries are infected, triggering the lose condition.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests the game's response to extreme rapid upgrading, simulating a player rapidly purchasing many upgrades.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Rapidly toggles between views and purchases as many upgrades as possible as quickly as DNA points allow.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should handle rapid purchasing without glitches, properly applying all upgrades and deducting DNA points.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests the game's balance by attempting to win with a suboptimal but viable strategy.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Balances purchases across all upgrade types rather than focusing on the optimal transmission-first approach.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should remain winnable but more challenging, demonstrating balanced gameplay mechanics.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
